## Overview

**v0.3.2 â€” App icon shipped on both platforms.**

The Tilda `~` (green) + Zig `Z` (orange) icon designed for the project
website is now baked into the app bundle on macOS and the executable
resource on Windows. Until now it had only lived in `docs/favicon.svg`
and only appeared on the GitHub Pages site; the binaries themselves
shipped with each platform's default placeholder (gray app icon on
macOS, generic console icon on Windows).

## Downloads

| Platform | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Windows | `tildaz-v0.3.2-windows.zip` | unzip anywhere. Includes `tildaz.exe` + bundled ConPTY runtime. |
| macOS (universal) | `tildaz-v0.3.2-macos.dmg` | Apple Silicon + Intel single binary. Drag `TildaZ.app` into Applications. macOS 13 Ventura or later. |

First-launch on macOS: the app is ad-hoc signed, so macOS may block the
first open. Right-click `TildaZ.app` â†’ Open, or run
`xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /Applications/TildaZ.app`. Then grant
Accessibility + Input Monitoring permissions once.

## What's new since v0.3.1

### macOS F1 hotkey reliability

- **F1 hotkey no longer dies after focus moves to apps like VS Code and back**
  ([#146](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/146)). macOS auto-disables
  active `CGEventTap` callbacks on response timeout or user-input race and
  signals the event by sending one of two special event types
  (`kCGEventTapDisabledByTimeout`, `kCGEventTapDisabledByUserInput`) to the
  callback. Until now we ignored those, so the tap would stay dead. The
  callback now re-arms the tap with `CGEventTapEnable(tap, true)` whenever
  it sees those events, the same pattern Ghostty / Alfred / Raycast / iTerm2
  all use. Re-enable events get a `[hotkey]` log line for monitoring.

### Application icon â€” both platforms

- **macOS** â€” `TildaZ.app/Contents/Resources/AppIcon.icns` shipped in
  the universal `.app` bundle, referenced from `Info.plist` via
  `CFBundleIconFile=AppIcon`. Visible in Finder, Dock (when shown),
  the DMG installer's drag-to-Applications view, and Cmd+Tab when the
  window is open. Built from `docs/favicon.svg` via `sips` â†’
  `.iconset` â†’ `iconutil`, ten resolutions from 16Ă—16 to 1024Ă—1024
  (`@2x` retina variants for each user-visible size).
- **Windows** â€” `IDI_ICON 1` in the executable PE resource. Visible in
  Explorer, the taskbar, and Alt+Tab. Built from `docs/favicon.svg`
  via `sips` â†’ six PNGs (16/32/48/64/128/256) â†’ multi-resolution
  `.ico` assembled with a small Python script.

The icon source remains a single `docs/favicon.svg`; the platform
artifacts (`dist/macos/AppIcon.icns`, `src/tildaz.ico`) are checked in
as build inputs so the GitHub Actions pipeline does not need extra
tooling on the runner. Regenerate from the SVG when the design
changes; see [#145](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/145) for
the exact sips / iconutil / Python invocations.

### Build infrastructure

- **Switched Setup Zig to `mlugg/setup-zig@v2`**
  ([#147](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/147)). The Windows
  runner hit `curl: (56) Recv failure: Connection was reset` against
  `ziglang.org` on multiple consecutive tries when cutting this
  release â€” mid-transfer RST that `curl --retry` does not catch. The
  user's browser had no issue with the same URL, pointing at an
  Azure â†” Cloudflare/Fastly peering quirk specific to GitHub-hosted
  Windows runners. ziglang.org publishes
  [`community-mirrors.txt`](https://ziglang.org/download/community-mirrors.txt)
  precisely because its main CDN is sponsored bandwidth not meant
  for high-volume CI traffic. `mlugg/setup-zig@v2` (since
  2025-09-09 commit `7465018`) has the community mirror list
  hardcoded and falls back automatically. Replaces 50+ lines of
  bash + curl with a 4-line action invocation, and decouples us
  from future Zig filename / mirror-list rotations. Windows job
  keeps `use-cache: false` and a separate step pinning
  `ZIG_GLOBAL_CACHE_DIR` to `D:\` to preserve the cross-drive
  `convertPathArg` workaround from #80.

## Known issues

Same as v0.3.1 â€” see [`v0.3.1.md`](v0.3.1.md). No new known issues
introduced by the icon work.

## Verification

- [ ] macOS: open the DMG; the staging window shows the green/orange
      Tilda + Z icon on `TildaZ.app`. After dragging into Applications,
      Finder and Dock display the same icon.
- [ ] macOS: `Cmd+Tab` while the dropdown window is visible shows the
      icon for tildaz.
- [ ] Windows: `tildaz.exe` in Explorer shows the green/orange icon
      (not the generic console icon). Taskbar and Alt+Tab match.
- [ ] DMG mounts on Apple Silicon and Intel, both slices present
      (`lipo -info /Applications/TildaZ.app/Contents/MacOS/tildaz`).
